conjecture_title: Open Problem: characterisation of ℓ-holed graphs for ℓ ∈ {4, 5, 6}
paper_title: Graphs with all holes the same length
summary: The source paper establishes a complete structural description of $\ell$-holed graphs for all $\ell \geq 7$ (Theorem 1.3) but explicitly leaves the cases $\ell \in \{4, 5, 6\}$ open. A related preprint (arXiv:2203.11571, 'When all holes have the same length') also restricts to $\ell \geq 7$ and does not address the open cases. No follow-up work providing a structural characterisation for $\ell = 4$, $5$, or $6$ was found in the indexed literature as of May 2026.
search_queries: graphs all holes same length structural description 4-holed 5-holed 6-holed Cook Seymour Trotignon Vušković
arxiv 2110.09970 citing papers l-holed graphs characterization
"5-holed graphs" OR "4-holed graphs" structural characterization holes length five four 2024 2025 2026
notes: arXiv:2203.11571 ('When all holes have the same length', submitted March 2022) addresses the same class of graphs but is also restricted to $\ell \geq 7$; it does not resolve the open problem for $\ell \in \{4, 5, 6\}$. The case $\ell = 5$ is noted in the source paper as connected to algebraic combinatorics and commutative algebra (via reference [12]), but no complete structural description exists. No citing papers resolving the conjecture were found after five web calls.
